category_title: Student with Duchenne muscular dystrophy exhibiting escape-motivated and attention-seeking disruptive behaviors
minimal_context: Philip is in language class and is asked by the teacher to write a language exercise.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Class, it is time for our language lesson. Please sit at your desks and write the language exercise.

STUDENT: (Leaves his seat without permission and wanders around the classroom annoying his classmates)

TUTOR: Philip, please go back to your seat and start writing.

STUDENT: No! (Looks angry and continues to wander around the classroom, avoiding the demanding task)

TUTOR: Come here, let's go back to your desk. (Tries to drive him to his seat)

STUDENT: You're stupid! (Falls on the ground at his attempt to escape from the teacher, cries, and refuses to stand up. He exhibits physical and verbal aggression, trying to throw nearby objects to hurt anyone who is nearby)

options.A: Approach Philip, try to calm him down, and have him sit up, ensuring that his classmates do not leave their activities to gather around him and give him attention.
options.B: Ask Philip, "Did you say foots or feet?" regarding the language exercise while he is on the floor during the lesson.
options.C: Tell Philip, "Don't say you can't, you can," while he is on the floor during the language lesson.
options.D: Provide explicit guidelines, expectations, and goals for the language exercise while he is on the floor during the lesson.
